headlines.title: Watch the moment Artemis II blasts off on historic mission
headlines.description: After delays and technical issues, the first crewed Moon mission in 50 years finally took off from Florida and is now in Earth's orbit.

headlines.title: Dream of space travel reignites with journey to circle the Moon
headlines.description: There was giddy euphoria at the Kennedy Space Center after the launch, writes the BBC's Pallab Ghosh.

headlines.title: Trump leaves key questions unanswered as he seeks to calm nerves over war
headlines.description: There were some glaring omissions in the president's primetime address, writes the BBC's Gary O'Donoghue.

headlines.title: UK to host virtual summit on reopening the Strait of Hormuz
headlines.description: Yvette Cooper is due to convene a virtual meeting of foreign ministers, though the US is not expected to attend.

headlines.title: Doctors lose new jobs package as strike to go ahead
headlines.description: The offer of 1,000 more training posts has been withdrawn after the union refused to scrap the planned six-day strike.

headlines.title: BBC sacked Scott Mills after learning alleged victim in police investigation was under 16
headlines.description: It comes as Mills released a statement saying he had "fully cooperated and responded" with the police investigation in 2018.

headlines.title: Petrol and diesel prices see biggest rise on record in March
headlines.description: The cost of filling a vehicle jumped at the sharpest pace than any other month, the RAC said.

headlines.title: Man dies in Athens storm as Saharan dust shrouds Crete
headlines.description: A man dies in flooding near Athens as rain lashes several regions in Greece, while a Saharan dust storm enveloped Crete.

headlines.title: New laws to make it easier to cancel subscriptions and get refunds
headlines.description: Consumers will be able to cancel unwanted subscriptions 'at the click of a button', the government said.

headlines.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX set to go public in $1 trillion share listing
headlines.description: The company’s public stock debut is set to be one of the most valuable in history, and could make Musk the world's first trillionaire.

headlines.title: Blind marathon runner to be guided by smart glasses
headlines.description: Clarke Reynolds will tackle the Brighton marathon with the help of a network of virtual guides.

headlines.title: Where responsibility lies when social media inspired mountaineers get into trouble
headlines.description: Who is responsible for safety in our mountains?

headlines.title: Motability drivers 'horrified' by compulsory black-box trackers
headlines.description: The boxes will monitor driving habits, such as speed and braking, and will provide a weekly rating.

headlines.title: Carol Kirkwood: Why the time is right for me to retire - and what's next
headlines.description: The weather forecast stalwart tells BBC News she's experienced loss in her life recently, but she's looking forward to what's to come.

headlines.title: 'We entered Race Across the World to honour dying wish'
headlines.description: Margo Oakley and her brother-in-law Mark Blythen bonded when their sister and wife Julia was terminally ill.

headlines.title: A year on: Four ways Trump's tariffs have changed the global economy
headlines.description: US tariffs stand at the highest rate in decades. But what has the impact been?

headlines.title: The Papers: 'Trump goes to war on Nato' and 'Scott breaks silence'
headlines.description: The US president's comments on considering withdrawing the US from Nato dominates Thursday's papers.

headlines.title: The over-70s making their comedy debut
headlines.description: A group of would-be performers have been learning the ropes of stand-up from a professional comedian.

headlines.title: Trump says Iran war objectives 'nearing completion' in address to nation
headlines.description: Trump's first televised address of the conflict with Iran did little to quell uneasy markets.

headlines.title: Oil jumps and shares fall after US president address
headlines.description: The US president says he will bring Iran "back to the Stone Age" but failed to say how the war will end.

headlines.title: Israel's perpetual war with Iran may be hard to win with military might alone
headlines.description: The war with Iran has not yet resolved any of Israel's regional conflicts in the way its prime minister suggested it would.

headlines.title: London Pride boss sacked after allegedly spending donations on perfume
headlines.description: The organisation has been in a protracted legal battle with its former chief executive.

headlines.title: Anonymous letter-writer could help solve who killed doctor and five children, police say
headlines.description: Police are keen to speak to the writer of an anonymous letter relating to the arson attack.

headlines.title: Magnitude 7.4 earthquake hits off Indonesia, killing one
headlines.description: The quake struck between the islands of Manado and Ternate. Tsunami warnings have now been lifted.

headlines.title: Utah teen identified as victim of serial killer Ted Bundy
headlines.description: New DNA testing has solved the 51-year-old case of murdered teenager Laura Ann Aime, according to investigators.

headlines.title: UK will seek closer ties with EU in light of Iran war, Starmer says
headlines.description: It comes as UK-US relations have been strained by the PM's refusal to be dragged further into the Iran war.

headlines.title: US Supreme Court appears sceptical of Trump plan to limit birthright citizenship
headlines.description: Trump attended the arguments in person, a rare move by a sitting president.

headlines.title: Woods declines US Ryder Cup captain role in 2027
headlines.description: Tiger Woods turns down the role of United States Ryder Cup captain next year in Ireland, the PGA of America announces, as he steps away from golf following last week's car crash.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Trump v Supreme Court: The battle over birthright citizenship
headlines.description: The case could affect the legal status of millions of Americans.

headlines.title: Isak to return to training this week - Slot
headlines.description: British record signing Alexander Isak will return to Liverpool training on Thursday, head coach Arne Slot confirms.

headlines.title: The remarkable rise of the only English coach in Tuchel's team
headlines.description: Ten years after playing for Accrington, Anthony Barry is hoping to help Thomas Tuchel win the World Cup for England.

headlines.title: Woods declines US Ryder Cup captain role in 2027
headlines.description: Tiger Woods turns down the role of United States Ryder Cup captain next year in Ireland, the PGA of America announces, as he steps away from golf following last week's car crash.

headlines.title: Bompastor angry with VAR after McCabe hair pull
headlines.description: Chelsea manager Sonia Bompastor criticises VAR as Arsenal's Katie McCabe avoids a red card for pulling Alyssa Thompson's hair.

headlines.title: Is success crucial? BBC Sport experts pick their player of the season
headlines.description: With award season just around the corner, BBC Sport experts pick their contenders for Premier League player of the year. Do you agree?

headlines.title: Who uses academy players most in Premier League - and does it matter?
headlines.description: As Max Dowman becomes the latest academy product to make an impact in the Premier League, BBC Sport looks at who uses their youngsters most.

headlines.title: What were the biggest moves of NFL free agency?
headlines.description: NFL teams splashed out billions in free agency, but what were the biggest moves and who were the biggest spenders? BBC Sport takes a look.
